title: 2000 vs 2002 Style DVD Packaging
wikitext:
[]: 2000 vs 2002 Style DVD Packaging.             Quote Post by PolarisX » Mon Jul 23, 2012 4:36 pm I have a "Perfect Collection" that I had lost Disk 2, so I hopped on Amazon and ordered a replacement. Has anyone noticed the front image is tweaked slightly, as well as the insert, and style of the physical case? One reads 2000 (the one I just got in) and the other reads 2002 (from my collection) The front image has the yellow design run up the progressive knife of Unit 2, as well as defining the color of the eyes. The insert as some definitions on the back instead of the menu listings, and the case itself has a large recycle symbol when you remove the insert. The colors are also more vibrant on the insert and front of the case, granted my collection is quite old now. I can take some photos, but just wanted to see if this was commonly known.

[]: Re: 2000 vs 2002 Style DVD Packaging.             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Jul 24, 2012 12:41 am PolarisX wrote: I have a "Perfect Collection" that I had lost Disk 2, so I hopped on Amazon and ordered a replacement. Has anyone noticed the front image is tweaked slightly, as well as the insert, and style of the physical case? One reads 2000 (the one I just got in) and the other reads 2002 (from my collection) The front image has the yellow design run up the progressive knife of Unit 2, as well as defining the color of the eyes. The insert as some definitions on the back instead of the menu listings, and the case itself has a large recycle symbol when you remove the insert. The colors are also more vibrant on the insert and front of the case, granted my collection is quite old now. I can take some photos, but just wanted to see if this was commonly known.

[]: Re: 2000 vs 2002 Style DVD Packaging.             Quote Post by PolarisX » Tue Jul 24, 2012 3:42 pm svenge wrote: Yeah, I knew about that. The covers for the first 2 volumes at the time they were originally released were done in a different style (mostly in terms of the spine and back) than the last 6 volumes. When ADV put out the Perfect Collection boxes, they redesigned the covers of Volumes 1 and 2 to be in the same style as Volumes 3+. The inserts for those two volumes were also changed in the same manner. While the front of the first volume was more visibly changed (e.g. the logo moving from the top to the bottom along with some general recomposition), the second volume's front did indeed change as well to be "less busy" as show below... Original Collection 0:2 "Perfect Collection" Collection 0:2 As for the differences in the physical cases themselves, that's just something that happens with choosing different replicators over time and really doesn't affect anything. Just count yourself lucky that you didn't have to replace Volume 1. The original release's DVD had tons of graphical overlays and terrible video quality; it was so bad that ADV had a different mastering house re-do it for the Perfect Collection boxes. I own a copy of both Volume 1 release variants (intentionally!) and I'm not exaggerating on the quality differential.

Article: 2000 vs 2002 Style DVD Packaging
: Yeah, I knew about that. The covers for the first 2 volumes at the time they were originally released were done in a different style (mostly in terms of the spine and back) than the last 6 volumes. When ADV put out the Perfect Collection boxes, they redesigned the covers of Volumes 1 and 2 to be in the same style as Volumes 3+. The inserts for those two volumes were also changed in the same manner. While the front of the first volume was more visibly changed (e.g. the logo moving from the top to the bottom along with some general recomposition), the second volume's front did indeed change as well to be "less busy" as show below... Original Collection 0:2 "Perfect Collection" Collection 0:2 As for the differences in the physical cases themselves, that's just something that happens with choosing different replicators over time and really doesn't affect anything. Just count yourself lucky that you didn't have to replace Volume 1. The original release's DVD had tons of graphical overlays and terrible video quality; it was so bad that ADV had a different mastering house re-do it for the Perfect Collection boxes. I own a copy of both Volume 1 release variants (intentionally!) and I'm not exaggerating on the quality differential.
